name: Incomplete Prompt — 'Run My Workflow' with No Name and No Input
expected_behavior: Step 1: Recognize the prompt is missing two essential pieces of information for `conductor workflow start`: (a) the workflow name (and possibly version), (b) the input payload.
Step 2: Do NOT make up a workflow name or fabricate inputs. Do NOT pick the first workflow alphabetically.
Step 3: Help the user help themselves: offer to list available workflows so they can pick. Show the command (`conductor workflow list --json` or `conductor workflow list`).
Step 4: Ask whether they want sync (`--sync`, blocks for completion) or async (default, returns immediately with workflow ID).
Step 5: If the user has run workflows recently in this session/profile, surface those names as likely candidates — but still ask, don't pick.

Step 6: Once a name is chosen, fetch the workflow definition to read the expected `inputParameters` schema — `conductor workflow get {name}` — so the agent can ask for the right input fields rather than letting the user guess.
Step 7: For input, offer both forms — `-i '{"...":"..."}'` for short inline JSON and `-f input.json` for larger payloads (per SKILL.md 'Creating workflows' section).
Step 8: End with the smallest possible next-step question — 'Which workflow? Want me to list them?' — not an explainer essay.
success_criteria: Agent does NOT invent a workflow name or pick one without confirmation
Agent does NOT fabricate input JSON
Agent offers to list available workflows so the user can pick — shows the actual command (`conductor workflow list` or `--json` variant)
Agent recognizes the inputs come from the workflow's defined `inputParameters` and offers to read the schema (`conductor workflow get {name}`) once a name is provided
Agent asks (or surfaces as a choice) sync (`--sync`) vs async — does not silently default to either
Agent's response leads with the missing-info question (which workflow? what input?) — any explanation of the workflow-start mechanics is secondary, not the main payload
Agent does not silently start *some* workflow
